title: Automatic Board connection to dashboard for new project boards created
excerpt:
can an ability to add new project boards be added to a dashboard automatically to decrease manual work and decrease reporting being inaccurate.

We have low level boards acting as projects. Having to manually add this b…

posts.author: DI-Sign-Group (Cameron McLean)
posts.cookedHtml:
• can an ability to add new project boards be added to a dashboard automatically to decrease manual work and decrease reporting being inaccurate.

We have low level boards acting as projects. Having to manually add this board to the capacity view is not ideal due to the fact adding this step can be easily overlooked or missed and this has a very large impact on the capacity board

Sorry not sure if this makes sense??

Regards

Cam McLean

posts.author: lindsaydreece (Lindsay Reece)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes!! We have the same need as well. It would be great to consolidate our low level boards into a dashboard view.

posts.author: jailynnhoops (Jai-Lynn Hoops)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! not only add though. But also to be able to add and then keep the filters that were on the original board when using a template!

posts.author: rehan.abdul (Rehan AQ | monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any update on this? Is this possible?

posts.author: AlinaNZ (Alina Panchenko)
posts.cookedHtml:
jailynnhoops:

only add though. But also to be able to add and then keep the filters that were on the original board when using a template!

Yes! Exactly the feature we need as well!
When a new Project board is created from a template board, we really need to have a way to connect it to all other dashboards and widgets, the same way as other ones.

Like an automation:

  1. Choose the “When status changes to something” trigger.
  2. Select the column that indicates when a board from the same template.
  3. In the “Then” section, choose the “Connect board to dashboard” action.
  4. Select all the dashboards you want to connect the board to.

posts.author: MitchB (Mitch Blobstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Are we sure this option exists? “In the “Then” section, choose the “Connect board to dashboard” action.”

I’ve never seen it.

posts.author: AlinaNZ (Alina Panchenko)
posts.cookedHtml: No, it doesn’t, it would be nice to have it.

posts.author: jvallee (Joshua Vallee)
posts.cookedHtml: Following up to see if this is on the radar for any future releases?

posts.author: cserra (Chris Serra)
posts.cookedHtml: I definitely need this! I actually just tried doing a support chat today about it. I have a high-level board for management to see an overview of projects (low level boards) that are upcoming, ongoing, and completed.

posts.author: josuexvolt (Josh Cruz)
posts.cookedHtml: This is something that a lot of clients have requested. If they want to see for example the pending tasks accross different boards in a single view. The high-level boards don’t work. They will need to manually connect these different projects to a dashboard

posts.author: carlylaine (Carly )
posts.cookedHtml:
yes!! I would really benefit from this feature as well.
“When new board is created in folder, connect board to dashboard.”

We have a reporting dashboard for projects, and a scheduling dashboard for projects. So this would save us a ton of time and ensure we don’t miss any project boards in our process.

posts.author: jhamb (Josh Beck)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be such a useful feature. We have too many lower level project boards to do make sure this is done reliably each time a new one is created. Any update?

posts.author: odelegacy (Calvin Ode)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this feature?

posts.author: ENies (Emily Nies)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d love to have this as well. I’m trying to set up something similar. When a new project we have to manually connect it to the “All Calendar” dashboard. An automation for something like this would be great.

posts.author: aquibk (Aquib Zafar)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone,

Is there any update or ETA for this ?

posts.author: danielle_arteaga (Danielle Arteaga)
posts.cookedHtml:
Also looking for an update. Would like a workflow to:

  1. Connect a new project board to a dashboard in the workspace
  2. Disconnect a project board from dashboard when the project is archived

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Danielle, am curious to know why you are archiving projects boards? Do you have a back up app for the data, or is it exported so it can be reported on long term?

posts.author: danielle_arteaga (Danielle Arteaga)
posts.cookedHtml: When I say “archive” here, I don’t mean the official archiving that MDC does. I mean that we have a folder called “Projects Archive” where we move completed projects. We retain them for historical reference, but in a folder that keeps the left-rail menu from getting cluttered with non-active projects. (This is particularly irksome when project boards are not added and cannot be sorted alphabetically except manually).

posts.author: garfield-labs (Snir Alon)
posts.cookedHtml:
It is actually quite possible to achieve this functionality with Same Item Multiple Boards.

Essentially, you can create a master board and send all of the items from the other boards to that master board. Then, simply create a dashboard and connect this master board to it.

Any changes from the project boards will be reflected in real time, and when you archive a board the items from that master board can be disconnected as well.

Let me know if you’d like a tutorial!

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml: Understood, we also do that. Do you have another Dashboard that connects the ‘Archived’ Projects? You can retain all your Projects Data with a monday app called Master Data in a single Master Board so you don’t have to keep disconnecting completed projects from a dashboard.

posts.author: AndreaRu (Andrea Rubiño)
posts.cookedHtml:
I need the same feature here!
Although I think it would be best to add a template board to a dashboard, and then, any project created with that template should be automatically created in the dashboard.
I think is best this way because you would want to have other teams or other kind of project outside your main dashboard.
Andrea

posts.author: SYNrtucker (Rae Tucker)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d love to learn a bit more about this option as I’m confused as to how it is different between adding new boards/projects to a master board vs adding new data sources to the dashboard. Unless new boards being added to the master board is automated, I’m not seeing or understanding where the time savings.

I have a folder in my workspace with a template and then all our projects. When a new project is created based on the template, it’s created at the bottom of the list. When a project is completed, we export the data and then archive the board.

So I’d want something that could not only add in new boards but also not break anything when boards get archived. Would the master board work like that at all?
